title: Ebenus humilis
intro: Ebenus humilis, also known as the dwarf bird's foot, is a plant species from the Fabaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation and fever, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. Its bioactive constituents include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Ebenus humilis is commonly prepared in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat digestive issues, reduce pain and inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Ebenus humilis is classified as a species within the genus Ebenus, and further classified as a member of the Fabaceae family of flowering plants. This family is commonly known as the legume or pea family. The Fabaceae family is one of the largest and most diverse plant families, containing over 19,000 species. Ebenus humilis is a type of shrub that is native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Africa. It is often found growing in dry, rocky areas and is known for its ability to thrive in challenging environments.

The plant is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer.
